# Section 15.7: Class Definitions

[Back to Section15](Section15.md) | [Back to Index](../Index.md)

> Last generated (UTC): 2026-05-24T13:24:47Z

JS2IL supports class declarations/expressions, methods (including async and generator methods), constructors (including derived constructors with super calls), class static blocks, private methods/accessors, and public/private instance/static fields with initializers. Computed class field names and literal/simple computed method names are supported; arbitrary runtime-computed class method names remain limited.

| Clause | Title | Status | Link |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 15.7 | Class Definitions | Supported with Limitations | [tc39.es](https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-class-definitions) |

## Support

Feature-level support tracking with repo test references and optional test262 evidence.

### 15.7.1 ([tc39.es](https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-class-definitions-static-semantics-early-errors))

| Feature name | Status | Test scripts | test262 evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Async instance method (async m() { await ... }) | Supported | [`Async_ClassMethod_SimpleAwait.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Async/JavaScript/Async_ClassMethod_SimpleAwait.js)<br>[`Async_ClassMethod_MultipleAwaits.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Async/JavaScript/Async_ClassMethod_MultipleAwaits.js)<br>[`Async_ClassMethod_WithThis.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Async/JavaScript/Async_ClassMethod_WithThis.js)<br>[`Async_ClassMethod_CallsOtherAsync.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Async/JavaScript/Async_ClassMethod_CallsOtherAsync.js) |  | Async class instance methods are compiled as resumable state machines and return a Promise; supports await points, multiple awaits, and correct this binding across suspension/resumption. |
| Async static method (static async m() { await ... }) | Supported | [`Async_StaticMethod_SimpleAwait.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Async/JavaScript/Async_StaticMethod_SimpleAwait.js) |  | Async class static methods are compiled as resumable state machines and return a Promise. |
| Class constructor restricted properties and prototype linkage | Supported with Limitations | [`poisoned-underscore-proto.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/expressions/class/JavaScript/poisoned-underscore-proto.js)<br>[`restricted-properties.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/expressions/class/JavaScript/restricted-properties.js) | suite `pr`<br>suite `nightly`<br>`test/language/expressions/class/poisoned-underscore-proto.js`<br>`test/language/expressions/class/restricted-properties.js` | Class constructor values expose Function.prototype inheritance and the covered restricted caller/arguments behavior without routing through poisoned Object.prototype.__proto__ accessors. |
| Class declaration (empty) | Supported | [`Classes_DeclareEmptyClass.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_DeclareEmptyClass.js) |  |  |
| Class inheritance from intrinsic Array (class X extends Array) | Supported with Limitations | [`Classes_IntrinsicInheritance_ExtendsArray_SuperLength.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_IntrinsicInheritance_ExtendsArray_SuperLength.js) |  | JS2IL resolves `extends Array` to the runtime CLR base type JavaScriptRuntime.Array. `super(...)` in derived constructors calls Array::.ctor() and then applies JS Array constructor semantics via Array.ConstructInto(args). Limitation: only `extends Array` is handled today; other intrinsic bases are not yet supported. |
| Class inheritance via extends (class B extends A) | Supported | [`Classes_Inheritance_SuperConstructor_Args.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_Inheritance_SuperConstructor_Args.js)<br>[`Classes_Inheritance_SuperMethodCall.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_Inheritance_SuperMethodCall.js)<br>[`Classes_Inheritance_NestedClassExtendsGlobal.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_Inheritance_NestedClassExtendsGlobal.js)<br>[`heritage-arrow-function.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/expressions/class/JavaScript/heritage-arrow-function.js)<br>[`class-definition-evaluation-empty-constructor-heritage-present.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/statements/class/subclass/JavaScript/class-definition-evaluation-empty-constructor-heritage-present.js)<br>[`class-definition-null-proto-super.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/statements/class/subclass/JavaScript/class-definition-null-proto-super.js)<br>[`default-constructor.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/statements/class/subclass/JavaScript/default-constructor.js)<br>[`arguments-callee.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/statements/class/strict-mode/JavaScript/arguments-callee.js)<br>[`class-field-is-observable-by-proxy.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/statements/class/elements/JavaScript/class-field-is-observable-by-proxy.js) | suite `pr`<br>suite `nightly`<br>`test/language/expressions/class/heritage-arrow-function.js`<br>`test/language/statements/class/subclass/class-definition-evaluation-empty-constructor-heritage-present.js`<br>`test/language/statements/class/subclass/class-definition-null-proto-super.js`<br>`test/language/statements/class/subclass/default-constructor.js` | Derived classes use the compiled base class as their CLR base type when resolvable, enabling direct base dispatch for instance methods. Nested classes that extend a globally-scoped class are now supported. Function-valued base constructors are supported for super(), including object/proxy replacement returns from the base constructor. The covered `test262` cases exercise implicit derived constructors, null-prototype heritage, and regular function-valued bases that forward through `super()`. |
| Class methods and constructors accessing ancestor scope variables (global, function, block) | Supported | [`Classes_ClassMethod_AccessGlobalVariable_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassMethod_AccessGlobalVariable_Log.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassMethod_AccessFunctionVariable_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassMethod_AccessFunctionVariable_Log.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassMethod_AccessFunctionVariableAndGlobalVariable_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassMethod_AccessFunctionVariableAndGlobalVariable_Log.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassMethod_AccessArrowFunctionVariable_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassMethod_AccessArrowFunctionVariable_Log.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassMethod_AccessArrowFunctionVariableAndGlobalVariable_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassMethod_AccessArrowFunctionVariableAndGlobalVariable_Log.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessGlobalVariable_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessGlobalVariable_Log.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessFunctionVariable_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessFunctionVariable_Log.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessFunctionVariableAndGlobalVariable_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessFunctionVariableAndGlobalVariable_Log.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessGlobalVariableAndParameterValue_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessGlobalVariableAndParameterValue_Log.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessFunctionVariableAndParameterValue_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessFunctionVariableAndParameterValue_Log.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessFunctionVariableAndGlobalVariableAndParameterValue_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_AccessFunctionVariableAndGlobalVariableAndParameterValue_Log.js) |  | Class methods and constructors can now access variables from all ancestor scopes (global, function, block), not just the global scope. Implementation walks the scope tree from the class's parent scope to root, building a complete ancestor chain. The scope array is passed as an argument to methods/constructors, enabling proper multi-level scope access. Supports regular functions, arrow functions, and constructor parameters. Fixed parameter indexing for constructors: when scopes are present, arg0=this, arg1=scopes[], parameters start at arg2. |
| Computed class accessor names (get ['x']() / static set [expr](v)) | Supported with Limitations | | Constructor default parameters | Supported | [`Classes_DefaultParameterValue_Constructor.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_DefaultParameterValue_Constructor.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassConstructor_ParameterDestructuring.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_ParameterDestructuring.js) |  | Class constructors support default parameter values and destructuring with defaults. Call sites validate argument count against min/max parameter bounds and pad missing optional arguments with ldnull. Default values are applied using starg IL pattern when arguments are null. Object destructuring parameters support default values (e.g., constructor({host="localhost", port=8080})) with conditional IL generation. |
| Constructor return value override semantics | Supported | [`Classes_Constructor_ExplicitReturnThis.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_Constructor_ExplicitReturnThis.js)<br>[`Classes_Constructor_ReturnObjectOverridesThis.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_Constructor_ReturnObjectOverridesThis.js) |  | Implements ECMAScript constructor behavior: `new C()` returns the constructed instance unless the constructor explicitly returns an object/function (which overrides). Returning a primitive does not override. |
| Constructor with multiple parameters; method uses fields | Supported | [`Classes_ClassConstructor_WithMultipleParameters.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_WithMultipleParameters.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassConstructor_TwoParams_AddMethod.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_TwoParams_AddMethod.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassConstructor_TwoParams_SubtractMethod.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_TwoParams_SubtractMethod.js) |  | Covers multi-parameter constructors and arithmetic in instance methods. |
| Constructor with parameter and this.field assignment; method reads field | Supported | [`Classes_ClassConstructor_Param_Field_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_Param_Field_Log.js) |  |  |
| Derived constructors: super(...) base constructor call | Supported | [`Classes_Inheritance_SuperConstructor_Args.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_Inheritance_SuperConstructor_Args.js)<br>[`arguments-callee.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/statements/class/strict-mode/JavaScript/arguments-callee.js)<br>[`class-field-is-observable-by-proxy.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/statements/class/elements/JavaScript/class-field-is-observable-by-proxy.js) |  | super(...) supports compiled class bases, Array intrinsic bases, and regular function-valued bases. For regular function bases, the base constructor is invoked with derived-constructor semantics and a returned object/proxy initializes the derived this binding; public field initialization targets that resolved receiver. |
| Derived constructors: this before super throws ReferenceError | Supported | [`Classes_Inheritance_ThisBeforeSuper_Throws.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_Inheritance_ThisBeforeSuper_Throws.js)<br>[`constructor-this-tdz-during-initializers.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/expressions/class/JavaScript/constructor-this-tdz-during-initializers.js) |  | Derived constructor this remains in TDZ before super(); arrows created before super capture the deferred this binding and observe the initialized receiver once super() completes before instance field initializers. |
| Generator instance method (*m() { yield ... }) | Supported | [`Generator_ClassMethod_SimpleYield.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Generator/JavaScript/Generator_ClassMethod_SimpleYield.js)<br>[`Generator_ClassMethod_YieldAssign.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Generator/JavaScript/Generator_ClassMethod_YieldAssign.js)<br>[`Generator_ClassMethod_WithThis.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Generator/JavaScript/Generator_ClassMethod_WithThis.js)<br>[`gen-method-length-dflt.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/expressions/class/JavaScript/gen-method-length-dflt.js) |  | Generator class instance methods compile to a synchronous generator state machine and return a GeneratorObject; supports yield points, function-valued prototype method properties, Function.length metadata, and correct this binding across next() calls. yield* delegation support is tracked under Generator Function Definitions. |
| Generator static method (static *m() { yield ... }) | Supported | [`Generator_StaticMethod_SimpleYield.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Generator/JavaScript/Generator_StaticMethod_SimpleYield.js) |  | Generator class static methods compile to a synchronous generator state machine and return a GeneratorObject. yield* delegation support is tracked under Generator Function Definitions. |
| Identifier-named class instance/static accessors (get x() / set x(v)) | Supported with Limitations | [`Classes_AccessorMethods_InstanceAndStatic.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_AccessorMethods_InstanceAndStatic.js) |  | Identifier-named instance and static accessors compile and dispatch correctly for normal property reads/writes, including accessor this binding and static access through the class object. Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor() correctly identifies getter/setter descriptors on class prototypes and static class objects. Static accessor methods use the correct CLR static ABI. Private accessors are compiled as hidden callable members. More complex runtime-computed accessor keys still have limitations. |
| Instance field initializer (public property default) | Supported | [`Classes_ClassProperty_DefaultAndLog.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassProperty_DefaultAndLog.js) |  | Emitted by assigning defaults in the generated .ctor. |
| Instance method (declare and call) | Supported | [`Classes_ClassWithMethod_HelloWorld.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassWithMethod_HelloWorld.js)<br>[`gen-method-length-dflt.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/expressions/class/JavaScript/gen-method-length-dflt.js) |  | Instance methods are available through normal direct dispatch and as non-enumerable configurable writable function-valued properties on the class prototype. |
| Instance method default parameters | Supported | [`Classes_DefaultParameterValue_Method.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_DefaultParameterValue_Method.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassMethod_ParameterDestructuring.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassMethod_ParameterDestructuring.js) |  | Class instance methods support default parameter values and destructuring with defaults. Methods are registered in ClassRegistry with min/max parameter counts. Call sites validate argument count ranges and pad missing optional parameters with ldnull before callvirt. Object destructuring parameters support default values with conditional IL generation. |
| Instance method implicit fallthrough return is undefined | Supported | [`Classes_Method_DefaultReturnUndefined.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_Method_DefaultReturnUndefined.js) |  | Class/instance methods that reach the end without an explicit return evaluate to JS undefined (represented as CLR null), matching ECMAScript semantics. |
| Private instance field (#) with helper method access | Supported | [`Classes_ClassPrivateField_HelperMethod_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassPrivateField_HelperMethod_Log.js) |  | Generated as a private .NET field with a mangled name; accessible only within the class. |
| Private methods and accessors (#m() {}, get #x(), set #x(v)) | Supported | `tests/Js2IL.Tests/ValidatorTests.cs`<br>[`Classes_ClassPrivateMethodAndAccessor_Log.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassPrivateMethodAndAccessor_Log.js) |  | Private methods and private accessors are compiled as hidden callable members distinct from private field storage, so they are not surfaced through normal property enumeration or lookup. |
| Static field initializer (static property default) | Supported | [`Classes_ClassWithStaticProperty_DefaultAndLog.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassWithStaticProperty_DefaultAndLog.js) |  | Emitted as a static field initialized in a synthesized .cctor; accessed via ldsfld. |
| Static method (declare and call) | Supported | [`Classes_ClassWithStaticMethod_HelloWorld.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassWithStaticMethod_HelloWorld.js)<br>[`fields-multiple-definitions-static-private-methods-proxy.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Test262.Tests/language/expressions/class/elements/JavaScript/fields-multiple-definitions-static-private-methods-proxy.js) |  | Static methods are available through normal direct dispatch and as non-enumerable configurable writable function-valued properties on the class constructor value. Static private method calls enforce the declaring-class receiver brand, including proxy mismatch rejection. |
| super.m(...) base method call | Supported | [`Classes_Inheritance_SuperMethodCall.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_Inheritance_SuperMethodCall.js) |  |  |

### 15.7.2 ([tc39.es](https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-static-semantics-classelementkind))

| Feature name | Status | Test scripts | test262 evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Class elements: methods vs. fields vs. constructors | Supported with Limitations | [`Classes_DeclareEmptyClass.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_DeclareEmptyClass.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassWithMethod_HelloWorld.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassWithMethod_HelloWorld.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassProperty_DefaultAndLog.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassProperty_DefaultAndLog.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassWithStaticMethod_HelloWorld.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassWithStaticMethod_HelloWorld.js)<br>[`Classes_ClassWithStaticProperty_DefaultAndLog.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassWithStaticProperty_DefaultAndLog.js) |  | JS2IL supports constructors, identifier/private-named methods and accessors, static blocks, and identifier/private-identifier fields. Literal/simple computed names are compiled onto the generated class/field surface; arbitrary runtime-computed method keys still have limitations. |

### 15.7.3 ([tc39.es](https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-static-semantics-constructormethod))

| Feature name | Status | Test scripts | test262 evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ConstructorMethod: explicit and implicit constructors | Supported | [`Classes_ClassConstructor_WithMultipleParameters.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassConstructor_WithMultipleParameters.js)<br>[`Classes_Inheritance_SuperConstructor_Args.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_Inheritance_SuperConstructor_Args.js) |  | Supports explicit constructors and synthesizes implicit constructors (including derived implicit super() insertion). |

Invalid private identifier usage is not fully validated per spec; some cases fail during compilation when registry resolution cannot find the field. |

| Identifier/private-identifier field definitions are emitted as CLR fields. Covered declaration and expression cases resolve identifier, null, string-literal, and numeric-literal computed field keys onto the generated class surface; dynamically computed field keys still use the generic runtime property path. |

### 15.7.11 ([tc39.es](https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-runtime-semantics-classstaticblockdefinitionevaluation))

| Feature name | Status | Test scripts | test262 evidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Class static blocks (static { ... }) | Supported | `tests/Js2IL.Tests/ValidatorTests.cs`<br>[`Classes_ClassStaticBlock_DeclarationOrder.js`](../../../tests/Js2IL.Tests/Classes/JavaScript/Classes_ClassStaticBlock_DeclarationOrder.js) |  | Static blocks are emitted into the synthesized class .cctor and execute once in declaration order relative to static field initialization. Class identifier bindings in static blocks correctly use the SSA-map (post-assignment) value, avoiding spurious TDZ errors during static initialization. |
